Peat bog at twilight, standing water pooled between grass tussocks, mist rising knee-deep around sedge clumps.
Flooded forest where birch trees stand in black water, fog threading silently between pale trunks.
Marsh path of rotting planks disappearing into thick fog, boards slick with moss and decay.
Swamp at dusk, cypress trees draped in Spanish moss, still dark water mirroring fading purple light.
Foggy river bend where brown water vanishes into white mist, far bank completely invisible beyond.
Dead forest in bog, skeletal white birch trunks standing in ink-black water, fog obscuring everything.
Moorland at night, treeless rolling hills, fog glowing faintly from something pulsing beneath the surface.
Abandoned stone causeway across marsh, rocks sinking into mud, fog erasing all destination ahead.
Pond choked with lily pads, dark water barely visible beneath vegetation, thick evening mist rising.
Forest creek crossing where fog pools thickest, water gurgling invisibly somewhere below the white.
Salt marsh at sea's edge, tidal pools and fog merging into uniform grey, no horizon.
Willow grove bordering bog, trailing branches vanishing downward into rolling fog below hanging leaves.
Fen at midnight, reeds standing in shallow water, mist swirling between stalks like living things.
Mangrove swamp at dusk, twisted roots emerging from brackish water, fog clinging to arching branches.
Quaking bog surface, sphagnum moss trembling underfoot, fog so thick trees appear as dark suggestions.
Oxbow lake in evening, stagnant water reflecting nothing, fog settling heavy across glassy surface.
Alder carr swamp, clustered trees in standing water, twilight mist weaving through tangled undergrowth below.
Cattail marsh stretching endlessly, brown seedheads emerging from fog, no land visible anywhere beyond.
Foggy wetland where river splits into braided channels, water disappearing into white in all directions.
Bottomland forest floor flooded ankle-deep, fog drifting between massive oak trunks in growing darkness.
Tidal slough at evening, mudflats half-submerged, fog rolling in with salt-scented water from sea.
Bog hollow where cranberry vines spread across dark pools, mist concealing how deep water goes.
Swamp clearing where cypress knees protrude from water, fog settling between them like snow drifts.
Marsh island of rotting vegetation barely above waterline, fog erasing everything beyond five feet away.
Twilight wetland where boundaries blur, water becomes fog becomes sky, everything grey and merging.
